hypothesis: If LoQI's stereochemistry-aware graph augmentation encoding R/S and E/Z configurations is extended to include axial chirality (e.g., atropisomers) and planar chirality through analogous directed-edge representations, then the resulting model will achieve stereochemical accuracy exceeding 0.95 for these non-tetrahedral chiral classes on drug-like molecules containing biaryl or paracyclophane scaffolds, as measured against DFT-optimized reference structures, because the architecture-agnostic nature of the augmentation strategy demonstrated for tetrahedral and double-bond stereocenters should generalize to any stereochemical element expressible as a local topological relationship in the molecular graph.
